verses.latin_vulgate: Ut ergo cognovit Jesus quia audierunt pharisæi quod Jesus plures discipulos facit, et baptizat, quam Joannes
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς οὖν ἔγνω ὁ κύριος ὅτι ἤκουσαν οἱ φαρισαῖοι ὅτι ἰησοῦς πλείονας μαθητὰς ποιεῖ καὶ βαπτίζει ἢ ἰωάννης
verses.king_james: When therefore the Lord knew how the Pharisees had heard that Jesus made and baptized more disciples than John,

verses.latin_vulgate: (quamquam Jesus non baptizaret, sed discipuli ejus),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καίτοιγε ἰησοῦς αὐτὸς οὐκ ἐβάπτιζεν ἀλλ᾽ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: (Though Jesus himself baptized not, but his disciples,)

verses.latin_vulgate: reliquit Judæam, et abiit iterum in Galilæam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀφῆκεν τὴν ἰουδαίαν καὶ ἀπῆλθεν πάλιν εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν
verses.king_james: He left Judaea, and departed again into Galilee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Oportebat autem eum transire per Samariam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔδει δὲ αὐτὸν διέρχεσθαι διὰ τῆς σαμαρείας
verses.king_james: And he must needs go through Samaria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit ergo in civitatem Samariæ, quæ dicitur Sichar, juxta prædium quod dedit Jacob Joseph filio suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔρχεται οὖν εἰς πόλιν τῆς σαμαρείας λεγομένην συχὰρ πλησίον τοῦ χωρίου ὃ ἔδωκεν ἰακὼβ ἰωσὴφ τῷ υἱῷ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Then cometh he to a city of Samaria, which is called Sychar, near to the parcel of ground that Jacob gave to his son Joseph.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erat autem ibi fons Jacob. Jesus ergo fatigatus ex itinere, sedebat sic supra fontem. Hora erat quasi sexta.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦν δὲ ἐκεῖ πηγὴ τοῦ ἰακώβ ὁ οὖν ἰησοῦς κεκοπιακὼς ἐκ τῆς ὁδοιπορίας ἐκαθέζετο οὕτως ἐπὶ τῇ πηγῇ ὥρα ἦν ὡσεὶ ἕκτη
verses.king_james: Now Jacob's well was there. Jesus therefore, being wearied with *his* journey, sat thus on the well: *and* it was about the sixth hour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit mulier de Samaria haurire aquam. Dicit ei Jesus: Da mihi bibere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔρχεται γυνὴ ἐκ τῆς σαμαρείας ἀντλῆσαι ὕδωρ λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς δός μοι πιεῖν
verses.king_james: There cometh a woman of Samaria to draw water: Jesus saith unto her, Give me to drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: (Discipuli enim ejus abierant in civitatem ut cibos emerent.)
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ γὰρ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ ἀπεληλύθεισαν εἰς τὴν πόλιν ἵνα τροφὰς ἀγοράσωσιν
verses.king_james: (For his disciples were gone away unto the city to buy meat.)

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ergo ei mulier illa Samaritana: Quomodo tu, Judæus cum sis, bibere a me poscis, quæ sum mulier Samaritana? non enim coutuntur Judæi Samaritanis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει οὖν αὐτῷ ἡ γυνὴ ἡ σαμαρεῖτις, πῶς σὺ ἰουδαῖος ὢν παρ᾽ ἐμοῦ πιεῖν αἰτεῖς οὔσης γυναικὸς σαμαρείτιδος οὐ γὰρ συγχρῶνται ἰουδαῖοι σαμαρείταις
verses.king_james: Then saith the woman of Samaria unto him, How is it that thou, being a Jew, askest drink of me, which am a woman of Samaria? for the Jews have no dealings with the Samaritans.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit ei: Si scires donum Dei, et quis est qui dicit tibi: Da mihi bibere, tu forsitan petisses ab eo, et dedisset tibi aquam vivam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῇ εἰ ᾔδεις τὴν δωρεὰν τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τίς ἐστιν ὁ λέγων σοι, δός μοι πιεῖν, σὺ ἂν ᾔτησας αὐτὸν καὶ ἔδωκεν ἄν σοι ὕδωρ ζῶν
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto her, If thou knewest the gift of God, and who it is that saith to thee, Give me to drink; thou wouldest have asked of him, and he would have given thee living water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei mulier: Domine, neque in quo haurias habes, et puteus altus est: unde ergo habes aquam vivam?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ἡ γυνή κύριε οὔτε ἄντλημα ἔχεις καὶ τὸ φρέαρ ἐστὶν βαθύ πόθεν οὖν ἔχεις τὸ ὕδωρ τὸ ζῶν
verses.king_james: The woman saith unto him, Sir, thou hast nothing to draw with, and the well is deep: from whence then hast thou that living water?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid tu major es patre nostro Jacob, qui dedit nobis puteum, et ipse ex eo bibit, et filii ejus, et pecora ejus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ σὺ μείζων εἶ τοῦ πατρὸς ἡμῶν ἰακώβ ὃς ἔδωκεν ἡμῖν τὸ φρέαρ καὶ αὐτὸς ἐξ αὐτοῦ ἔπιεν καὶ οἱ υἱοὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ τὰ θρέμματα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Art thou greater than our father Jacob, which gave us the well, and drank thereof himself, and his children, and his cattle?

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit Jesus, et dixit ei: Omnis qui bibit ex aqua hac, sitiet iterum;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ὁ ἰησοῦς καὶ εἶπεν αὐτῇ πᾶς ὁ πίνων ἐκ τοῦ ὕδατος τούτου διψήσει πάλιν
verses.king_james: Jesus answered and said unto her, Whosoever drinketh of this water shall thirst again:

verses.latin_vulgate: qui autem biberit ex aqua quam ego dabo ei, non sitiet in æternum: sed aqua quam ego dabo ei, fiet in eo fons aquæ salientis in vitam æternam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃς δ᾽ ἂν πίῃ ἐκ τοῦ ὕδατος οὗ ἐγὼ δώσω αὐτῷ οὐ μὴ διψήσῃ εἰς τὸν αἰῶνα ἀλλὰ τὸ ὕδωρ ὃ δώσω αὐτῷ γενήσεται ἐν αὐτῷ πηγὴ ὕδατος ἁλλομένου εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον
verses.king_james: But whosoever drinketh of the water that I shall give him shall never thirst; but the water that I shall give him shall be in him a well of water springing up into everlasting life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ad eum mulier: Domine, da mihi hanc aquam, ut non sitiam, neque veniam huc haurire.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει πρὸς αὐτὸν ἡ γυνή κύριε δός μοι τοῦτο τὸ ὕδωρ ἵνα μὴ διψῶ μηδὲ έρχωμαι ἐνθάδε ἀντλεῖν
verses.king_james: The woman saith unto him, Sir, give me this water, that I thirst not, neither come hither to draw.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Vade, voca virum tuum, et veni huc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς, ὕπαγε φώνησον τὸν ἄνδρα σου καὶ ἐλθὲ ἐνθάδε
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto her, Go, call thy husband, and come hither.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit mulier, et dixit: Non habeo virum. Dicit ei Jesus: Bene dixisti, quia non habeo virum;
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπεκρίθη ἡ γυνὴ καὶ εἶπεν οὐκ ἔχω ἄνδρα λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς καλῶς εἶπας ὅτι ἄνδρα οὐκ ἔχω
verses.king_james: The woman answered and said, I have no husband. Jesus said unto her, Thou hast well said, I have no husband:

verses.latin_vulgate: quinque enim viros habuisti, et nunc, quem habes, non est tuus vir: hoc vere dixisti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πέντε γὰρ ἄνδρας ἔσχες καὶ νῦν ὃν ἔχεις οὐκ ἔστιν σου ἀνήρ τοῦτο ἀληθὲς εἴρηκας
verses.king_james: For thou hast had five husbands; and he whom thou now hast is not thy husband: in that saidst thou truly.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei mulier: Domine, video quia propheta es tu.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ἡ γυνή κύριε θεωρῶ ὅτι προφήτης εἶ σύ
verses.king_james: The woman saith unto him, Sir, I perceive that thou art a prophet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Patres nostri in monte hoc adoraverunt, et vos dicitis, quia Jerosolymis est locus ubi adorare oportet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ πατέρες ἡμῶν ἐν τούτῳ τῷ ὄρει προσεκύνησαν καὶ ὑμεῖς λέγετε ὅτι ἐν ἱεροσολύμοις ἐστὶν ὁ τόπος ὅπου δεῖ προσκυνεῖν
verses.king_james: Our fathers worshipped in this mountain; and ye say, that in Jerusalem is the place where men ought to worship.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Mulier, crede mihi, quia venit hora, quando neque in monte hoc, neque in Jerosolymis adorabitis Patrem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς γύναι πίστευσον μοι ὅτι ἔρχεται ὥρα ὅτε οὔτε ἐν τῷ ὄρει τούτῳ οὔτε ἐν ἱεροσολύμοις προσκυνήσετε τῷ πατρί
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, believe me, the hour cometh, when ye shall neither in this mountain, nor yet at Jerusalem, worship the Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos adoratis quod nescitis: nos adoramus quod scimus, quia salus ex Judæis est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς προσκυνεῖτε ὃ οὐκ οἴδατε ἡμεῖς προσκυνοῦμεν ὃ οἴδαμεν ὅτι ἡ σωτηρία ἐκ τῶν ἰουδαίων ἐστίν
verses.king_james: Ye worship ye know not what: we know what we worship: for salvation is of the Jews.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed venit hora, et nunc est, quando veri adoratores adorabunt Patrem in spiritu et veritate. Nam et Pater tales quærit, qui adorent eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἔρχεται ὥρα καὶ νῦν ἐστιν ὅτε οἱ ἀληθινοὶ προσκυνηταὶ προσκυνήσουσιν τῷ πατρὶ ἐν πνεύματι καὶ ἀληθείᾳ καὶ γὰρ ὁ πατὴρ τοιούτους ζητεῖ τοὺς προσκυνοῦντας αὐτόν
verses.king_james: But the hour cometh, and now is, when the true worshippers shall worship the Father in spirit and in truth: for the Father seeketh such to worship him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Spiritus est Deus: et eos qui adorant eum, in spiritu et veritate oportet adorare.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πνεῦμα ὁ θεός καὶ τοὺς προσκυνοῦντας αὐτὸν ἐν πνεύματι καὶ ἀληθείᾳ δεῖ προσκυνεῖν
verses.king_james: God *is* a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship *him* in spirit and in truth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei mulier: Scio quia Messias venit (qui dicitur Christus): cum ergo venerit ille, nobis annuntiabit omnia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ἡ γυνή οἶδα ὅτι μεσσίας ἔρχεται ὁ λεγόμενος χριστός ὅταν ἔλθῃ ἐκεῖνος ἀναγγελεῖ ἡμῖν πάντα
verses.king_james: The woman saith unto him, I know that Messias cometh, which is called Christ: when he is come, he will tell us all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Ego sum, qui loquor te.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῇ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐγώ εἰμι ὁ λαλῶν σοι
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto her, I that speak unto thee am *he*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et continuo venerunt discipuli ejus, et mirabantur quia cum muliere loquebatur. Nemo tamen dixit: Quid quæris? aut, Quid loqueris cum ea?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐπὶ τούτῳ ἦλθον οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐθαύμασαν ὅτι μετὰ γυναικὸς ἐλάλει οὐδεὶς μέντοι εἶπεν τί ζητεῖς ἤ τί λαλεῖς μετ᾽ αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: And upon this came his disciples, and marvelled that he talked with the woman: yet no man said, What seekest thou? or, Why talkest thou with her?

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliquit ergo hydriam suam mulier, et abiit in civitatem, et dicit illis hominibus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀφῆκεν οὖν τὴν ὑδρίαν αὐτῆς ἡ γυνὴ καὶ ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὴν πόλιν καὶ λέγει τοῖς ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: The woman then left her waterpot, and went her way into the city, and saith to the men,

verses.latin_vulgate: Venite, et videte hominem qui dixit mihi omnia quæcumque feci: numquid ipse est Christus?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεῦτε ἴδετε ἄνθρωπον ὃς εἶπέν μοι πάντα ὅσα ἐποίησα μήτι οὗτός ἐστιν ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: Come, see a man, which told me all things that ever I did: is not this the Christ?

verses.latin_vulgate: Exierunt ergo de civitate et veniebant ad eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐξῆλθον οὖν ἐκ τῆς πόλεως καὶ ἤρχοντο πρὸς αὐτόν
verses.king_james: Then they went out of the city, and came unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interea rogabant eum discipuli, dicentes: Rabbi, manduca.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τῷ μεταξὺ ἠρώτων αὐτὸν οἱ μαθηταὶ λέγοντες ῥαββί φάγε
verses.king_james: In the mean while his disciples prayed him, saying, Master, eat.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ille autem dicit eis: Ego cibum habeo manducare, quem vos nescitis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ἐγὼ βρῶσιν ἔχω φαγεῖν ἣν ὑμεῖς οὐκ οἴδατε
verses.king_james: But he said unto them, I have meat to eat that ye know not of.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebant ergo discipuli ad invicem: Numquid aliquis attulit ei manducare?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγον οὖν οἱ μαθηταὶ πρὸς ἀλλήλους μή τις ἤνεγκεν αὐτῷ φαγεῖν
verses.king_james: Therefore said the disciples one to another, Hath any man brought him *ought* to eat?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit eis Jesus: Meus cibus est ut faciam voluntatem ejus qui misit me, ut perficiam opus ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐμὸν βρῶμά ἐστιν ἵνα ποιῶ τὸ θέλημα τοῦ πέμψαντός με καὶ τελειώσω αὐτοῦ τὸ ἔργον
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto them, My meat is to do the will of him that sent me, and to finish his work.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne vos dicitis quod adhuc quatuor menses sunt, et messis venit? Ecce dico vobis: levate oculos vestros, et videte regiones, quia albæ sunt jam ad messem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐχ ὑμεῖς λέγετε ὅτι ἔτι τετράμηνόν ἐστιν καὶ ὁ θερισμὸς ἔρχεται ἰδού, λέγω ὑμῖν ἐπάρατε τοὺς ὀφθαλμοὺς ὑμῶν καὶ θεάσασθε τὰς χώρας ὅτι λευκαί εἰσιν πρὸς θερισμόν ἤδη
verses.king_james: Say not ye, There are yet four months, and *then* cometh harvest? behold, I say unto you, Lift up your eyes, and look on the fields; for they are white already to harvest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et qui metit, mercedem accipit, et congregat fructum in vitam æternam: ut et qui seminat, simul gaudeat, et qui metit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ θερίζων μισθὸν λαμβάνει καὶ συνάγει καρπὸν εἰς ζωὴν αἰώνιον ἵνα καὶ ὁ σπείρων ὁμοῦ χαίρῃ καὶ ὁ θερίζων
verses.king_james: And he that reapeth receiveth wages, and gathereth fruit unto life eternal: that both he that soweth and he that reapeth may rejoice together.

verses.latin_vulgate: In hoc enim est verbum verum: quia alius est qui seminat, et alius est qui metit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν γὰρ τούτῳ ὁ λόγος ἐστὶν ὁ ἀληθινὸς ὅτι ἄλλος ἐστὶν ὁ σπείρων καὶ ἄλλος ὁ θερίζων
verses.king_james: And herein is that saying true, One soweth, and another reapeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego misi vos metere quod vos non laborastis: alii laboraverunt, et vos in labores eorum introistis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ ἀπέστειλα ὑμᾶς θερίζειν ὃ οὐχ ὑμεῖς κεκοπιάκατε ἄλλοι κεκοπιάκασιν καὶ ὑμεῖς εἰς τὸν κόπον αὐτῶν εἰσεληλύθατε
verses.king_james: I sent you to reap that whereon ye bestowed no labour: other men laboured, and ye are entered into their labours.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ex civitate autem illa multi crediderunt in eum Samaritanorum, propter verbum mulieris testimonium perhibentis: Quia dixit mihi omnia quæcumque feci.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐκ δὲ τῆς πόλεως ἐκείνης πολλοὶ ἐπίστευσαν εἰς αὐτὸν τῶν σαμαρειτῶν, διὰ τὸν λόγον τῆς γυναικὸς μαρτυρούσης ὅτι εἶπέν μοι πάντα ὅσα ἐποίησα
verses.king_james: And many of the Samaritans of that city believed on him for the saying of the woman, which testified, He told me all that ever I did.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum venissent ergo ad illum Samaritani, rogaverunt eum ut ibi maneret. Et mansit ibi duos dies.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς οὖν ἦλθον πρὸς αὐτὸν οἱ σαμαρεῖται, ἠρώτων αὐτὸν μεῖναι παρ᾽ αὐτοῖς καὶ ἔμεινεν ἐκεῖ δύο ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: So when the Samaritans were come unto him, they besought him that he would tarry with them: and he abode there two days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et multo plures crediderunt in eum propter sermonem ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ πολλῷ πλείους ἐπίστευσαν διὰ τὸν λόγον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And many more believed because of his own word;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mulieri dicebant: Quia jam non propter tuam loquelam credimus: ipsi enim audivimus, et scimus quia hic est vere Salvator mundi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τῇ τε γυναικὶ ἔλεγον ὅτι οὐκέτι διὰ τὴν σὴν λαλιὰν πιστεύομεν αὐτοὶ γὰρ ἀκηκόαμεν καὶ οἴδαμεν ὅτι οὗτός ἐστιν ἀληθῶς ὁ σωτὴρ τοῦ κόσμου ὁ χριστός
verses.king_james: And said unto the woman, Now we believe, not because of thy saying: for we have heard *him* ourselves, and know that this is indeed the Christ, the Saviour of the world.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post duos autem dies exiit inde, et abiit in Galilæam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ τὰς δύο ἡμέρας ἐξῆλθεν ἐκεῖθεν καὶ ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν
verses.king_james: Now after two days he departed thence, and went into Galilee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse enim Jesus testimonium perhibuit, quia propheta in sua patria honorem non habet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτὸς γὰρ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐμαρτύρησεν ὅτι προφήτης ἐν τῇ ἰδίᾳ πατρίδι τιμὴν οὐκ ἔχει
verses.king_james: For Jesus himself testified, that a prophet hath no honour in his own country.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum ergo venisset in Galilæam, exceperunt eum Galilæi, cum omnia vidissent quæ fecerat Jerosolymis in die festo: et ipsi enim venerant ad diem festum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε οὖν ἦλθεν εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν ἐδέξαντο αὐτὸν οἱ γαλιλαῖοι πάντα ἑωρακότες ἃ ἐποίησεν ἐν ἱεροσολύμοις ἐν τῇ ἑορτῇ καὶ αὐτοὶ γὰρ ἦλθον εἰς τὴν ἑορτήν
verses.king_james: Then when he was come into Galilee, the Galilaeans received him, having seen all the things that he did at Jerusalem at the feast: for they also went unto the feast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit ergo iterum in Cana Galilææ, ubi fecit aquam vinum. Et erat quidam regulus, cujus filius infirmabatur Capharnaum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦλθεν οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς πάλιν εἰς τὴν κανὰ τῆς γαλιλαίας ὅπου ἐποίησεν τὸ ὕδωρ οἶνον καὶ ἦν τις βασιλικὸς οὗ ὁ υἱὸς ἠσθένει ἐν καπερναούμ
verses.king_james: So Jesus came again into Cana of Galilee, where he made the water wine. And there was a certain nobleman, whose son was sick at Capernaum.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hic cum audisset quia Jesus adveniret a Judæa in Galilæam, abiit ad eum, et rogabat eum ut descenderet, et sanaret filium ejus: incipiebat enim mori.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτος ἀκούσας ὅτι ἰησοῦς ἥκει ἐκ τῆς ἰουδαίας εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν ἀπῆλθεν πρὸς αὐτὸν καὶ ἠρώτα αὐτὸν, ἵνα καταβῇ καὶ ἰάσηται αὐτοῦ τὸν υἱόν ἤμελλεν γὰρ ἀποθνῄσκειν
verses.king_james: When he heard that Jesus was come out of Judaea into Galilee, he went unto him, and besought him that he would come down, and heal his son: for he was at the point of death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit ergo Jesus ad eum: Nisi signa et prodigia videritis, non creditis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἶπεν οὖν ὁ ἰησοῦς πρὸς αὐτόν ἐὰν μὴ σημεῖα καὶ τέρατα ἴδητε οὐ μὴ πιστεύσητε
verses.king_james: Then said Jesus unto him, Except ye see signs and wonders, ye will not believe.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ad eum regulus: Domine, descende priusquam moriatur filius meus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει πρὸς αὐτὸν ὁ βασιλικός κύριε κατάβηθι πρὶν ἀποθανεῖν τὸ παιδίον μου
verses.king_james: The nobleman saith unto him, Sir, come down ere my child die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicit ei Jesus: Vade, filius tuus vivit. Credidit homo sermoni quem dixit ei Jesus, et ibat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς πορεύου ὁ υἱός σου ζῇ καὶ ἐπίστευσεν ὁ ἄνθρωπος τῷ λόγῳ ᾦ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ἰησοῦς καὶ ἐπορεύετο
verses.king_james: Jesus saith unto him, Go thy way; thy son liveth. And the man believed the word that Jesus had spoken unto him, and he went his way.

verses.latin_vulgate: Jam autem eo descendente, servi occurrerunt ei, et nuntiaverunt dicentes, quia filius ejus viveret.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤδη δὲ αὐτοῦ καταβαίνοντος οἱ δοῦλοι αὐτοῦ ἀπήντησαν αὐτῷ καὶ ἀπήγγειλαν λέγοντες ὅτι ὁ παῖς σου ζῇ
verses.king_james: And as he was now going down, his servants met him, and told *him*, saying, Thy son liveth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interrogabat ergo horam ab eis in qua melius habuerit. Et dixerunt ei: Quia heri hora septima reliquit eum febris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπύθετο οὖν παρ᾽ αὐτῶν τὴν ὥραν ἐν ᾗ κομψότερον ἔσχεν καὶ εἶπον αὐτῷ ὅτι χθὲς ὥραν ἑβδόμην ἀφῆκεν αὐτὸν ὁ πυρετός
verses.king_james: Then enquired he of them the hour when he began to amend. And they said unto him, Yesterday at the seventh hour the fever left him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cognovit ergo pater, quia illa hora erat in qua dixit ei Jesus: Filius tuus vivit; et credidit ipse et domus ejus tota.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔγνω οὖν ὁ πατὴρ ὅτι ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ὥρᾳ ἐν ᾗ εἶπεν αὐτῷ ὁ ἰησοῦς ὅτι ὁ υἱός σου ζῇ καὶ ἐπίστευσεν αὐτὸς καὶ ἡ οἰκία αὐτοῦ ὅλη
verses.king_james: So the father knew that *it was* at the same hour, in the which Jesus said unto him, Thy son liveth: and himself believed, and his whole house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc iterum secundum signum fecit Jesus, cum venisset a Judæa in Galilæam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τοῦτο πάλιν δεύτερον σημεῖον ἐποίησεν ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐλθὼν ἐκ τῆς ἰουδαίας εἰς τὴν γαλιλαίαν
verses.king_james: This *is* again the second miracle *that* Jesus did, when he was come out of Judaea into Galilee.
